## Page 49

# CHAPTER 4
## COBIT GOVERNANCE AND MANAGEMENT OBJECTIVES—DETAILED GUIDANCE

<table>
  <tr>
    <td style="vertical-align: top;">
      **Domain:** Evaluate, Direct and Monitor<br>
      **Governance Objective:** EDM05 – Ensured Stakeholder Engagement
    </td>
    <td style="vertical-align: top;">
      **Focus Area:** COBIT Core Model
    </td>
  </tr>
</table>

### Description
Ensure that stakeholders are identified and engaged in the I&T governance system and that enterprise I&T performance and conformance measurement and reporting are transparent, with stakeholders approving the goals and metrics and necessary remedial actions.

### Purpose
Ensure that stakeholders are supportive of the I&T strategy and road map, communication to stakeholders is effective and timely, and the basis for reporting is established to increase performance. Identify areas for improvement, and confirm that I&T-related objectives and strategies are in line with the enterprise’s strategy.

The governance objective supports the achievement of a set of primary enterprise and alignment goals:

<table>
  <tr>
    <td style="vertical-align: top; width: 50%;">
      **Enterprise Goals**
      <ul>
        <li>• EG04 Quality of financial information</li>
        <li>• EG07 Quality of management information</li>
      </ul>
      **Example Metrics for Enterprise Goals**
      <table>
        <tr>
          <td>EG04</td>
          <td>
            a. Satisfaction survey of key stakeholders regarding the transparency, understanding and accuracy of enterprise financial information<br>
            b. Cost of noncompliance with finance-related regulations
          </td>
        </tr>
        <tr>
          <td>EG07</td>
          <td>
            a. Degree of board and executive management satisfaction with decision-making information<br>
            b. Number of incidents caused by incorrect business decisions based on inaccurate information<br>
            c. Time to provide information supporting effective business decisions<br>
            d. Timeliness of management information
          </td>
        </tr>
      </table>
    </td>
    <td style="vertical-align: top; width: 50%;">
      **Alignment Goals**
      <ul>
        <li>AG10 Quality of I&T management information</li>
      </ul>
      **Example Metrics for Alignment Goals**
      <table>
        <tr>
          <td>AG10</td>
          <td>
            a. Level of user satisfaction with quality, timeliness and availability of I&T-related management information, taking into account available resources<br>
            b. Ratio and extent of erroneous business decisions in which erroneous or unavailable I&T-related information was a key factor<br>
            c. Percentage of information meeting quality criteria
          </td>
        </tr>
      </table>
    </td>
  </tr>
</table>

### A. Component: Process

<table>
  <tr>
    <td style="vertical-align: top;">
      **Governance Practice**
      <br>
      **EDM05.01 Evaluate stakeholder engagement and reporting requirements.** Continually examine and evaluate current and future requirements for stakeholder engagement and reporting (including reporting mandated by regulatory requirements), and communication to other stakeholders. Establish principles for engaging and communicating with stakeholders.
    </td>
    <td style="vertical-align: top;">
      **Example Metrics**
      <ul>
        <li>a. Date of last revision to reporting requirements</li>
        <li>b. Percent of stakeholders covered in reporting requirements</li>
      </ul>
    </td>
  </tr>
</table>

<table>
  <thead>
    <tr>
      <th>Activities</th>
      <th>Capability Level</th>
    </tr>
  </thead>
  <tbody>
    <tr>
      <td>1. Identify all relevant I&T stakeholders within and outside the enterprise. Group stakeholders in stakeholder categories with similar requirements.</td>
      <td rowspan="4">2</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td>2. Examine and make judgment on the current and future mandatory reporting requirements relating to the use of I&T within the enterprise (regulation, legislation, common law, contractual), including extent and frequency.</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td>3. Examine and make judgment on the current and future communication and reporting requirements for other stakeholders relating to the use of I&T within the enterprise, including required level of involvement/consultation and extent of communication/level of detail and conditions.</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td>4. Maintain principles for communication with external and internal stakeholders, including communication formats and channels, and for stakeholder acceptance and sign-off of reporting.</td>
    </tr>
  </tbody>
</table>

<table>
  <tr>
    <td style="vertical-align: top;">
      **Related Guidance (Standards, Frameworks, Compliance Requirements)**
      <ul>
        <li>CMMI Cybermaturity Platform, 2018</li>
      </ul>
    </td>
    <td style="vertical-align: top;">
      **Detailed Reference**
      <ul>
        <li>SR.DR Direct Stakeholder Communication and Reporting</li>
      </ul>
    </td>
  </tr>
</table>

&lt;page_number&gt;49&lt;/page_number&gt;

# COBIT® 2019 FRAMEWORK: GOVERNANCE AND MANAGEMENT OBJECTIVES

**Evaluate, Direct and Monitor**

<table>
  <thead>
    <tr>
      <th colspan="3">A. Component: Process (cont.)</th>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <th>Governance Practice</th>
      <th>Example Metrics</th>
      <th></th>
    </tr>
  </thead>
  <tbody>
    <tr>
      <td>
        **EDM05.02 Direct stakeholder engagement, communication and reporting.**<br>
        Ensure the establishment of effective stakeholder involvement, communication and reporting, including mechanisms for ensuring the quality and completeness of information, overseeing mandatory reporting, and creating a communication strategy for stakeholders.
      </td>
      <td>
        a. Number of breaches of mandatory reporting requirements<br>
        b. Stakeholder satisfaction with communication and reporting
      </td>
      <td></td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td>
        **Activities**
      </td>
      <td></td>
      <td>
        Capability Level
      </td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td>
        1. Direct the establishment of the consultation and communication strategy for external and internal stakeholders.<br>
        2. Direct the implementation of mechanisms to ensure that information meets all criteria for mandatory I&T reporting requirements for the enterprise.<br>
        3. Establish mechanisms for validation and approval of mandatory reporting.<br>
        4. Establish reporting escalation mechanisms.
      </td>
      <td></td>
      <td>2</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td>
        **Related Guidance (Standards, Frameworks, Compliance Requirements)**
      </td>

        **Detailed Reference**
      </td>
      <td></td>
    </tr>

      <td>Part 5.5: Stakeholder relationships—Principle 16</td>
      <td></td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td>King IV Report on Corporate Governance for South Africa, 2016</td>
      <td>Part 5.2: Strategy, performance and reporting—Principle 5</td>
      <td></td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td>National Institute of Standards and Technology Framework for Improving Critical Infrastructure Cybersecurity V1.1, April 2018</td>
      <td>3.3 Communicating Cybersecurity Requirements with Stakeholders</td>
      <td></td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td>
        **Governance Practice**
      </td>
      <td>
        **Example Metrics**
      </td>
      <td></td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td>
        **EDM05.03 Monitor stakeholder engagement.**<br>
        Monitor stakeholder engagement levels and the effectiveness of stakeholder communication. Assess mechanisms for ensuring accuracy, reliability and effectiveness, and ascertain whether the requirements of different stakeholders in terms of reporting and communication are met.
      </td>
      <td>
        a. Level of stakeholder engagement with enterprise I&T<br>
        b. Percent of reports containing inaccuracies<br>
        c. Percent of reports delivered on time
      </td>
      <td></td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td>
        **Activities**
      </td>
      <td></td>
      <td>
        Capability Level
      </td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td>
        1. Periodically assess the effectiveness of the mechanisms for ensuring the accuracy and reliability of mandatory reporting.<br>
        2. Periodically assess the effectiveness of the mechanisms for, and outcomes from, involvement of and communication with external and internal stakeholders.<br>
        3. Determine whether the requirements of different stakeholders are met and assess stakeholder engagement levels.
      </td>
      <td></td>
      <td>4</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td>
        **Related Guidance (Standards, Frameworks, Compliance Requirements)**
      </td>
      <td>
        **Detailed Reference**
      </td>
      <td></td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td>CMMI Cybermaturity Platform, 2018</td>
      <td>SR.MC Monitor Stakeholder Communication</td>
      <td></td>
    </tr>

</table>

## Page 55

# CHAPTER 4
## COBIT GOVERNANCE AND MANAGEMENT OBJECTIVES—DETAILED GUIDANCE

<table>
  <tr>
    <td style="vertical-align: top;">
      **Domain:** Align, Plan and Organize<br>
      **Management Objective:** APO01 – Managed I&T Management Framework
    </td>
    <td style="vertical-align: top;">
      **Focus Area:** COBIT Core Model
    </td>
  </tr>
</table>

### Description
Design the management system for enterprise I&T based on enterprise goals and other design factors. Based on this design, implement all required components of the management system.

### Purpose
Implement a consistent management approach for enterprise governance requirements to be met, covering governance components such as management processes; organizational structures; roles and responsibilities; reliable and repeatable activities; information items; policies and procedures; skills and competencies; culture and behavior; and services, infrastructure and applications.

The management objective supports the achievement of a set of primary enterprise and alignment goals:

<table>
  <tr>
    <td style="vertical-align: top; padding-right: 20px;">
      **Enterprise Goals**
      <ul>
        <li>• EG03 Compliance with external laws and regulations</li>
        <li>• EG08 Optimization of internal business process functionality</li>
        <li>• EG11 Compliance with internal policies</li>
        <li>• EG12 Managed digital transformation programs</li>
      </ul>
      **Example Metrics for Enterprise Goals**
      <table>
        <tr>
          <td>EG03</td>
          <td>
            <ul>
              <li>a. Cost of regulatory noncompliance, including settlements and fines</li>
              <li>b. Number of regulatory noncompliance issues causing public comment or negative publicity</li>
              <li>c. Number of noncompliance matters noted by regulators</li>
              <li>d. Number of regulatory noncompliance issues relating to contractual agreements with business partners</li>
            </ul>
          </td>
        </tr>
        <tr>
          <td>EG08</td>
          <td>
            <ul>
              <li>a. Satisfaction levels of board and executive management with business process capabilities</li>
              <li>b. Satisfaction levels of customers with service delivery capabilities</li>
              <li>c. Satisfaction levels of suppliers with supply chain capabilities</li>
            </ul>
          </td>
        </tr>
        <tr>
          <td>EG11</td>
          <td>
            <ul>
              <li>a. Number of incidents related to noncompliance to policy</li>
              <li>b. Percent of stakeholders who understand policies</li>
              <li>c. Percent of policies supported by effective standards and working practices</li>
            </ul>
          </td>
        </tr>
        <tr>
          <td>EG12</td>
          <td>
            <ul>
              <li>a. Number of programs on time and within budget</li>
              <li>b. Percent of stakeholders satisfied with program delivery</li>
              <li>c. Percent of business transformation programs stopped</li>
              <li>d. Percent of business transformation programs with regular reported status updates</li>
            </ul>
          </td>
        </tr>
      </table>
    </td>
    <td style="vertical-align: top; padding-left: 20px;">
      **Alignment Goals**
      <ul>
        <li>• AG03 Realized benefits from I&T-enabled investments and services portfolio</li>
        <li>• AG11 I&T compliance with internal policies</li>
      </ul>
      **Example Metrics for Alignment Goals**
      <table>
        <tr>
          <td>AG03</td>
          <td>
            <ul>
              <li>a. Percent of I&T-enabled investments for which claimed benefits in the business case are met or exceeded</li>
              <li>b. Percent of I&T services for which expected benefits (as stated in service level agreements) are realized</li>
            </ul>
          </td>
        </tr>
        <tr>
          <td>AG11</td>
          <td>
            <ul>
              <li>a. Number of incidents related to noncompliance with I&T-related policies.</li>
              <li>b. Number of exceptions to internal policies</li>
              <li>c. Frequency of policy review and update</li>
            </ul>
          </td>
        </tr>
      </table>
    </td>
  </tr>
</table>

&lt;page_number&gt;55&lt;/page_number&gt;

## Page 56

# COBIT® 2019 FRAMEWORK: GOVERNANCE AND MANAGEMENT OBJECTIVES

## A. Component: Process

<table>
  <thead>
    <tr>
      <th>Management Practice</th>
      <th>Example Metrics</th>
    </tr>
  </thead>
  <tbody>
    <tr>
      <td><strong>APO01.01 Design the management system for enterprise I&amp;T.</strong><br>Design a management system tailored to the needs of the enterprise. Management needs of the enterprise are defined through the use of the goals cascade and by application of design factors. Ensure the governance components are integrated and aligned with the enterprise's governance and management philosophy and operating style.</td>
      <td>a. Number of formal sign-offs by applicable governance structures of the priority objectives for the I&amp;T management system<br>b. Percent of governance components integrated and aligned with the enterprise's governance and management philosophy and operating style</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td colspan="2"><strong>Activities</strong></td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td colspan="2">1. Obtain an understanding of the enterprise vision, direction and strategy as well as the current enterprise context and challenges.</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td colspan="2">2. Consider the enterprise's internal environment, including management culture and philosophy, risk tolerance, security and privacy policy, ethical values, code of conduct, accountability, and requirements for management integrity.</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td colspan="2">3. Apply the COBIT goals cascade and design factors to the enterprise strategy and context to decide on priorities for the management system and, thus, for implementation of management objective priorities.</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td colspan="2">4. Validate selected priorities for implementation of management objectives with industry-specific good practices or requirements (e.g., industry-specific regulations) and with appropriate governance structures.</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td colspan="2"><strong>Related Guidance (Standards, Frameworks, Compliance Requirements)</strong></td>
      <td><strong>Detailed Reference</strong></td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td>COSO Enterprise Risk Management, June 2017</td>
      <td>7. Strategy and Objective-Setting—Principle 9</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td>ISO/IEC 27001:2013/Cor.2:2015(E)</td>
      <td>International standard for establishing, implementing and maintaining a management system (all chapters)</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td>ITIL V3, 2011</td>
      <td>Service Strategy, 2.3 Governance and management systems</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td><strong>Management Practice</strong></td>
      <td><strong>Example Metrics</strong></td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td><strong>APO01.02 Communicate management objectives, direction and decisions made.</strong><br>Communicate awareness and promote understanding of alignment and I&amp;T objectives to stakeholders throughout the enterprise. Communicate at regular intervals on important I&amp;T-related decisions and their impact for the organization.</td>
      <td>a. Frequency of communication on management objectives and direction for I&amp;T<br>b. Assigned responsibility for sending out regular communications</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td colspan="2"><strong>Activities</strong></td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td colspan="2">1. Provide sufficient and skilled resources to support the communication process.</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td colspan="2">2. Define ground rules for communication by identifying communication needs and implementing plans based on those needs, considering top-down, bottom-up and horizontal communication.</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td colspan="2">3. Continuously communicate I&amp;T objectives and direction. Ensure that communication is supported by executive management in actions and words, using all available channels.</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td colspan="2">4. Ensure the information communicated encompasses a clearly articulated mission, service objectives, internal controls, quality, code of ethics/conduct, policies and procedures, roles and responsibilities, etc. Communicate the information at the appropriate level of detail for respective audiences within the enterprise.</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td colspan="2"><strong>Related Guidance (Standards, Frameworks, Compliance Requirements)</strong></td>
      <td><strong>Detailed Reference</strong></td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td>No related guidance for this component</td>
      <td></td>
    </tr>
  </tbody>
</table>

&lt;page_number&gt;56&lt;/page_number&gt;

## Page 57

CHAPTER 4
COBIT GOVERNANCE AND MANAGEMENT OBJECTIVES—DETAILED GUIDANCE

A. Component: Process (cont.)

<table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th>Management Practice</th>
<th>Example Metrics</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td><strong>APO01.03 Implement management processes (to support the achievement of governance and management objectives).</strong><br>Define target process capability levels and implementation priority based on the management system design.</td>
<td>a. Number of priority processes to be implemented or improved to meet the target capability level<br>b. Number of metrics defined for follow-up of successful process implementation</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

<table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th>Activities</th>
<th>Capability Level</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>1. Develop the I&T governance target process model specific to the organization, based on the selection of priority management objectives (output of goals cascade and design factors exercise).</td>
<td>2</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>2. Analyze the gap between the target process model for the organization and current practices and activities.</td>
<td>3</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>3. Draft a road map for implementation of missing process practices and activities. Use practice metrics to follow up on successful implementation.</td>
<td>4</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

<table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th>Related Guidance (Standards, Frameworks, Compliance Requirements)</th>
<th>Detailed Reference</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>No related guidance for this management practice</td>
<td></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

<table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th>Management Practice</th>
<th>Example Metrics</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td><strong>APO01.04 Define and implement the organizational structures.</strong><br>Put in place the required internal and extended organizational structures (e.g., committees) per the management system design, enabling effective and efficient decision making. Ensure that required technology and information knowledge is included in the composition of management structures.</td>
<td>a. Level of executive satisfaction with management decision making<br>b. Number of decisions that could not be resolved within management structures and were escalated to governance structures</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

<table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th>Activities</th>
<th>Capability Level</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>1. Identify decisions required for the achievement of enterprise outcomes and the I&T strategy and for the management and execution of I&T services.</td>
<td>2</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>2. Involve stakeholders who are critical to decision making (accountable, responsible, consulted or informed).</td>
<td></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>3. Define the scope, focus, mandate and responsibilities of each function within the I&T-related organization, in line with governance direction.</td>
<td></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>4. Define the scope of internal and external functions, internal and external roles, and capabilities and decision rights required to cover all practices, including those performed by third parties.</td>
<td>3</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>5. Align the I&T-related organization with enterprise architecture organizational models.</td>
<td></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>6. Establish an I&T steering committee (or equivalent) composed of executive, business and I&T management to track status of projects, resolve resource conflicts, and monitor service levels and service improvements.</td>
<td></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>7. Provide guidelines for each management structure (including mandate, objectives, meeting attendees, timing, tracking, supervision and oversight) as well as required inputs for and expected outcomes of meetings.</td>
<td></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>8. Regularly verify the adequacy and effectiveness of the organizational structures.</td>
<td>4</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

<table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th>Related Guidance (Standards, Frameworks, Compliance Requirements)</th>
<th>Detailed Reference</th>

## Page 58

# COBIT® 2019 FRAMEWORK: GOVERNANCE AND MANAGEMENT OBJECTIVES

<table>
  <thead>
    <tr>
      <th colspan="3">A. Component: Process (cont.)</th>
    </tr>
  </thead>
  <tbody>
    <tr>
      <td rowspan="15"><strong>Align, Plan and Organize</strong></td>
      <td><strong>Management Practice</strong></td>
      <td><strong>Example Metrics</strong></td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td><strong>APO01.05 Establish roles and responsibilities.</strong><br>Define and communicate roles and responsibilities for enterprise I&T, including authority levels, responsibilities and accountability.</td>
      <td>a. Number of I&T-related roles assigned to individuals<br>b. Number of completed role descriptions</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td><strong>Activities</strong></td>
      <td><strong>Capability Level</strong></td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td>1. Establish, agree on and communicate I&T-related roles and responsibilities for all personnel in the enterprise, in alignment with business needs and objectives. Clearly delineate responsibilities and accountabilities, especially for decision making and approvals.</td>
      <td>2</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td>2. Consider requirements from enterprise and I&T service continuity when defining roles, including staff back-up and cross-training requirements.</td>
      <td></td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td>3. Provide input to the I&T service continuity process by maintaining up-to-date contact information and role descriptions in the enterprise.</td>
      <td></td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td>4. Include specific requirements in role and responsibility descriptions regarding adherence to management policies and procedures, the code of ethics, and professional practices.</td>
      <td></td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td>5. Ensure that accountability is defined through roles and responsibilities.</td>
      <td></td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td>6. Structure roles and responsibilities to reduce the possibility for a single role to compromise a critical process.</td>
      <td></td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td>7. Implement adequate supervisory practices to ensure that roles and responsibilities are properly exercised, to assess whether all personnel have sufficient authority and resources to execute their roles and responsibilities, and generally to review performance. The level of supervision should be aligned with the sensitivity of the position and extent of assigned responsibilities.</td>
      <td>3</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td><strong>Related Guidance (Standards, Frameworks, Compliance Requirements)</strong></td>
      <td><strong>Detailed Reference</strong></td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td>No related guidance for this management practice</td>
      <td></td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td><strong>Management Practice</strong></td>
      <td><strong>Example Metrics</strong></td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td><strong>APO01.06 Optimize the placement of the IT function.</strong><br>Position the IT capabilities in the overall organizational structure to reflect the strategic importance and operational dependency of IT within the enterprise. The reporting line of the CIO and representation of IT within senior management should be commensurate with the importance of I&T within the enterprise.</td>
      <td>a. Number of key stakeholders that have signed off on the placement of the IT function<br>b. Percent of stakeholders with a favorable opinion of the placement of the IT function</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td><strong>Activities</strong></td>
      <td><strong>Capability Level</strong></td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td>1. Understand context for the placement of the IT function, including assessment of enterprise strategy and operating model (centralized, federated, decentralized, hybrid), importance of I&T, and sourcing situation and options.</td>
      <td>3</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td>2. Identify, evaluate and prioritize options for organizational placement, sourcing and operating models.</td>
      <td></td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td>3. Define placement of the IT function and obtain agreement.</td>
      <td></td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td><strong>Related Guidance (Standards, Frameworks, Compliance Requirements)</strong></td>
      <td><strong>Detailed Reference</strong></td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td>ISO/IEC 27002:2013/Cor.2:2015(E)</td>
      <td>8.2 Information classification</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td><strong>Management Practice</strong></td>
      <td><strong>Example Metrics</strong></td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td><strong>APO01.07 Define information (data) and system ownership.</strong><br>Define and maintain responsibilities for ownership of information (data) and information systems. Ensure that owners classify information and systems and protect them in line with their classification.</td>
      <td>a. Percent of data assets with clearly defined owners<br>b. Percent of information systems with clearly defined owners<br>c. Percent of information items classified according to the agreed classification levels</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td><strong>Activities</strong></td>
      <td><strong>Capability Level</strong></td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td>1. Provide guidelines to ensure appropriate and consistent enterprisewide classification of information items.</td>
      <td>3</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td>2. Create and maintain an inventory of information (systems and data) that includes a listing of owners, custodians and classifications. Include systems that are outsourced and those for which ownership should stay within the enterprise.</td>
      <td></td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td>3. Assess and distinguish between critical (high value) and noncritical data, information and systems. Ensure appropriate protection for each category.</td>
      <td></td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td><strong>Related Guidance (Standards, Frameworks, Compliance Requirements)</strong></td>
      <td><strong>Detailed Reference</strong></td>

<table>

<tbody>
<tr>
<td><strong>APO01.08 Define target skills and competencies.</strong><br>Define the required skills and competencies to achieve relevant management objectives.</td>
<td>a. Number of staff who have attended training or awareness sessions for selected skills, competencies, desired behaviors<br>b. Percent of staff with required skills and competencies aligned to selected management objectives</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><strong>Activities</strong></td>
<td></td>
<td><strong>Capability Level</strong></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>1. Identify the required skills and competencies to achieve selected management objectives.</td>
<td></td>
<td rowspan="2">2</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>2. Analyze the gap between target skills and capabilities for the enterprise and current skills of the workforce. Refer to APO07–Managed Human Resources for skills development and management practices.</td>
<td></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><strong>Related Guidance (Standards, Frameworks, Compliance Requirements)</strong></td>
<td><strong>Detailed Reference</strong></td>

</tr>
<tr>
<td><strong>APO01.09 Define and communicate policies and procedures.</strong><br>Put in place procedures to maintain compliance with and performance measurement of policies and other components of the control framework. Enforce the consequences of noncompliance or inadequate performance. Track trends and performance and consider these in the future design and improvement of the control framework.</td>
<td>a. Percent of active policies and procedures that are documented and up to date<br>b. Number of staff aware and able to demonstrate competency with respect to policies and procedures</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><strong>Activities</strong></td>
<td></td>
<td><strong>Capability Level</strong></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>1. Create a set of policies to drive IT control expectations on relevant key topics such as quality, security, privacy, internal controls, usage of I&T assets, ethics and intellectual property (IP) rights.</td>
<td></td>
<td rowspan="3">3</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>2. Roll out and enforce I&T policies uniformly for all relevant staff so they are built into, and become integral parts of, enterprise operations.</td>
<td></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>3. Evaluate and update the policies at least yearly to accommodate changing operating or business environments.</td>
<td></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><strong>Related Guidance (Standards, Frameworks, Compliance Requirements)</strong></td>
<td><strong>Detailed Reference</strong></td>
<td></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td colspan="3">No related guidance for this management practice</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><strong>Management Practice</strong></td>
<td><strong>Example Metrics</strong></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><strong>APO01.10 Define and implement infrastructure, services and applications to support the governance and management system.</strong><br>Define and implement infrastructure, services and applications to support the governance and management system (e.g., architecture repositories, risk management system, project management tools, cost-tracking tools and incident monitoring tools).</td>
<td>a. Number of tools selected to support priority processes<br>b. Adequacy/coverage by the tools of key I&T processes<br>c. Satisfaction of recipients with the accuracy, completeness and timeliness of information<br>d. Percent of stakeholder satisfaction with tools selected to support their needs</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><strong>Activities</strong></td>
<td></td>
<td><strong>Capability Level</strong></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>1. Identify priority management objectives that may be achieved by automating services, applications or infrastructure.</td>
<td></td>
<td rowspan="3">2</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>2. Select and implement the most appropriate tools and communicate to stakeholders.</td>
<td></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>3. Provide training on selected tools, as required.</td>
<td></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><strong>Related Guidance (Standards, Frameworks, Compliance Requirements)</strong></td>
<td><strong>Detailed Reference</strong></td>
<td></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td colspan="3">No related guidance for this management practice</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

Align, Plan and Organize

&lt;page_number&gt;59&lt;/page_number&gt;

## Page 60

# COBIT® 2019 FRAMEWORK: GOVERNANCE AND MANAGEMENT OBJECTIVES

## A. Component: Process (cont.)

<table>
  <thead>
    <tr>
      <th>Management Practice</th>
      <th>Example Metrics</th>
    </tr>
  </thead>
  <tbody>
    <tr>
      <td><strong>APO01.11 Manage continual improvement of the I&T management system.</strong><br>Continually improve processes and other management system components to ensure that they can deliver against governance and management objectives. Consider COBIT implementation guidance, emerging standards, compliance requirements, automation opportunities and the feedback of stakeholders.</td>
      <td>a. Date of last updates to the framework and components<br>b. Number of I&T-related loss exposures due to inadequacies in the design of the control environment</td>
    </tr>
  </tbody>
</table>

### Activities

<table>
  <thead>
    <tr>
      <th>Activities</th>
      <th>Capability Level</th>
    </tr>
  </thead>
  <tbody>
    <tr>
      <td>1. Regularly assess performance of framework components and take appropriate action.</td>
      <td>4</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td>2. Identify business-critical processes based on performance and conformance drivers and related risk. Assess capability and identify improvement targets. Analyze gaps in capability and control. Identify options for improving or redesigning the process.</td>
      <td></td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td>3. Prioritize initiatives for improvement based on potential benefits and costs. Implement agreed improvements, operate as normal business practice, and set performance goals and metrics to enable monitoring of improvements.</td>
      <td>5</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td>4. Consider ways to improve efficiency and effectiveness (e.g., through training, documentation, standardization and/or process automation).</td>
      <td></td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td>5. Apply quality management practices to update the process.</td>
      <td></td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td>6. Retire outdated governance components (processes, information items, policies, etc.).</td>
      <td></td>
    </tr>
  </tbody>
</table>

<table>
  <thead>
    <tr>
      <th>Related Guidance (Standards, Frameworks, Compliance Requirements)</th>
      <th>Detailed Reference</th>
    </tr>
  </thead>
  <tbody>
    <tr>
      <td>ITIL V3, 2011</td>
      <td>Continual Service Improvement, 4.1 The 7-Step Improvement Process</td>
    </tr>
  </tbody>
</table>

## Page 65

# CHAPTER 4
## COBIT GOVERNANCE AND MANAGEMENT OBJECTIVES—DETAILED GUIDANCE

<table>
  <tr>
    <td style="vertical-align: top;">
      **Domain:** Align, Plan and Organize<br>
      **Management Objective:** APO02 – Managed Strategy
    </td>
    <td style="vertical-align: top;">
      **Focus Area:** COBIT Core Model
    </td>
  </tr>
</table>

### Description
Provide a holistic view of the current business and I&T environment, the future direction, and the initiatives required to migrate to the desired future environment. Ensure that the desired level of digitization is integral to the future direction and the I&T strategy. Assess the organization’s current digital maturity and develop a road map to close the gaps. With the business, rethink internal operations as well as customer-facing activities. Ensure focus on the transformation journey across the organization. Leverage enterprise architecture building blocks, governance components and the organization’s ecosystem, including externally provided services and related capabilities, to enable reliable but agile and efficient response to strategic objectives.

### Purpose
Support the digital transformation strategy of the organization and deliver the desired value through a road map of incremental changes. Use a holistic I&T approach, ensuring that each initiative is clearly connected to an overarching strategy. Enable change in all different aspects of the organization, from channels and processes to data, culture, skills, operating model and incentives.

The management objective supports the achievement of a set of primary enterprise and alignment goals:

<table>
  <thead>
    <tr>
      <th>Enterprise Goals</th>
      <th>Alignment Goals</th>
    </tr>
  </thead>
  <tbody>
    <tr>
      <td>
        • EG01 Portfolio of competitive products and services<br>
        • EG05 Customer-oriented service culture<br>
        • EG08 Optimization of internal business process functionality<br>
        • EG12 Managed digital transformation programs
      </td>
      <td>
        AG08 Enabling and supporting business processes by integrating applications and technology
      </td>
    </tr>
  </tbody>
</table>

<table>
  <thead>
    <tr>
      <th>Example Metrics for Enterprise Goals</th>
      <th>Example Metrics for Alignment Goals</th>
    </tr>
  </thead>
  <tbody>
    <tr>
      <td>
        EG01
        <ul>
          <li>a. Percent of products and services that meet or exceed targets in revenues and/or market share</li>
          <li>b. Percent of products and services that meet or exceed customer satisfaction targets</li>
          <li>c. Percent of products and services that provide competitive advantage</li>
          <li>d. Time to market for new products and services</li>
        </ul>
      </td>
      <td>
        AG08
        <ul>
          <li>a. Time to execute business services or processes</li>
          <li>b. Number of I&T-enabled business programs delayed or incurring additional cost due to technology-integration issues</li>
          <li>c. Number of business process changes that need to be delayed or reworked because of technology-integration issues</li>
          <li>d. Number of applications or critical infrastructures operating in silos and not integrated</li>
        </ul>
      </td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td>
        EG05
        <ul>
          <li>a. Number of customer service disruptions</li>
          <li>b. Percent of business stakeholders satisfied that customer service delivery meets agreed levels</li>
          <li>c. Number of customer complaints</li>
          <li>d. Trend of customer satisfaction survey results</li>
        </ul>
      </td>
      <td></td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td>
        EG08
        <ul>
          <li>a. Satisfaction levels of board and executive management with business process capabilities</li>
          <li>b. Satisfaction levels of customers with service delivery capabilities</li>
          <li>c. Satisfaction levels of suppliers with supply chain capabilities</li>
        </ul>
      </td>
      <td></td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td>
        EG12
        <ul>
          <li>a. Number of programs on time and within budget</li>
          <li>b. Percent of stakeholders satisfied with program delivery</li>
          <li>c. Percent of business transformation programs stopped</li>
          <li>d. Percent of business transformation programs with regular reported status updates</li>
        </ul>
      </td>
      <td></td>
    </tr>
  </tbody>
</table>

&lt;page_number&gt;65&lt;/page_number&gt;

## Page 66

# COBIT® 2019 FRAMEWORK: GOVERNANCE AND MANAGEMENT OBJECTIVES

## A. Component: Process

<table>
  <thead>
    <tr>
      <th>Management Practice</th>
      <th>Example Metrics</th>
    </tr>
  </thead>
  <tbody>
    <tr>
      <td><strong>APO02.01 Understand enterprise context and direction.</strong><br>Understand the enterprise context (industry drivers, relevant regulations, basis for competition), its current way of working and its ambition level in terms of digitization.</td>
      <td>a. Level of understanding within I&T management of current enterprise organization and context<br>b. Level of knowledge within I&T management of enterprise goals and direction<br>c. Level of understanding of key stakeholders for I&T and their detailed requirements</td>
    </tr>
  </tbody>
</table>

### Activities

1. Develop and maintain an understanding of the external environment of the enterprise.
2. Develop and maintain an understanding of the current way of working, including the operational environment, enterprise architecture (business, information, data, applications and technology domains), enterprise culture and current challenges.
3. Develop and maintain an understanding of future enterprise direction, including enterprise strategy, goals and objectives. Understand the ambition level of the enterprise in terms of digitization, which may include a range of increasingly aspirational goals, from cutting costs, increasing customer centricity, or getting to market faster by digitizing internal operations, to creating entirely new revenue streams from new business models (e.g., platform business).
4. Identify key stakeholders and obtain insight on their requirements.

<table>
  <thead>
    <tr>
      <th>Related Guidance (Standards, Frameworks, Compliance Requirements)</th>
      <th>Detailed Reference</th>
    </tr>
  </thead>
  <tbody>
    <tr>
      <td>COSO Enterprise Risk Management, June 2017</td>
      <td>7. Strategy and Objective-Setting—Principle 6</td>
    </tr>
  </tbody>
</table>

### Management Practice

**APO02.02 Assess current capabilities, performance and digital maturity of the enterprise.**
Assess the performance of current I&T services and develop an understanding of current business and I&T capabilities (both internal and external). Assess current digital maturity of the enterprise and its appetite for change.

<table>
  <thead>
    <tr>
      <th>Activities</th>
      <th>Capability Level</th>
    </tr>
  </thead>
  <tbody>
    <tr>
      <td>1. Develop a baseline of current business and I&T capabilities and services. Include assessment of externally provisioned services, governance of I&T, and enterprisewide I&T-related skills and competencies.</td>
      <td>2</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td>2. Assess digital maturity across different dimensions (e.g., ability of leadership to leverage technology, level of accepted technology risk, approach to innovation, culture and knowledge level of users). Assess appetite for change.</td>
      <td>3</td>
    </tr>
  </tbody>
</table>

<table>
  <thead>
    <tr>
      <th>Related Guidance (Standards, Frameworks, Compliance Requirements)</th>
      <th>Detailed Reference</th>
    </tr>
  </thead>
  <tbody>
    <tr>
      <td>COSO Enterprise Risk Management, June 2017</td>
      <td>7. Strategy and Objective-Setting—Principle 6; 9. Review and Revision—Principle 15</td>
    </tr>
  </tbody>
</table>

### Management Practice

**APO02.03 Define target digital capabilities.**
Based on the understanding of enterprise context and direction, define the target I&T products and services and required capabilities. Consider reference standards, best practices and validated emerging technologies.

<table>
  <thead>
    <tr>
      <th>Activities</th>
      <th>Capability Level</th>
    </tr>
  </thead>
  <tbody>
    <tr>
      <td>1. Summarize enterprise context and direction and identify specific I&T aspects of enterprise strategy (e.g., digitizing processes, implementing new technology, supporting legacy architecture, applying new digital business models, developing digital product portfolio, etc.).</td>
      <td>2</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td>2. Define high-level I&T objectives and goals and specify their contribution to enterprise objectives.</td>
      <td></td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td>3. Detail required I&T services and products to realize enterprise objectives. Consider validated emerging technology or innovation ideas, reference standards, competitor business and I&T capabilities, comparative benchmarks of good practice, and emerging I&T service provision.</td>
      <td>3</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td>4. Determine I&T capabilities, methodologies and organizational approaches required to realize the defined I&T product and service portfolio. Consider different development methodologies (Agile, scrum, waterfall, bimodal IT), depending on business requirements. Consider how each could help realize I&T objectives.</td>
      <td></td>
    </tr>
  </tbody>
</table>

<table>
  <thead>
    <tr>
      <th>Related Guidance (Standards, Frameworks, Compliance Requirements)</th>
      <th>Detailed Reference</th>
    </tr>
  </thead>
  <tbody>
    <tr>
      <td>No related guidance for this management practice</td>
      <td></td>
    </tr>
  </tbody>
</table>

&lt;page_number&gt;66&lt;/page_number&gt;

## Page 67

CHAPTER 4
COBIT GOVERNANCE AND MANAGEMENT OBJECTIVES—DETAILED GUIDANCE

A. Component: Process (cont.)

<table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th>Management Practice</th>
<th>Example Metrics</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td><strong>APO02.04 Conduct a gap analysis.</strong><br>Identify gaps between current and target environments and describe the high-level changes in the enterprise architecture.</td>
<td>a. Number of high-impact changes required in the different enterprise architecture domains<br>b. Number of significant gaps between current environment and good practices</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><strong>Activities</strong></td>
<td></td>
<td><strong>Capability Level</strong></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>1. Identify all gaps and changes required to realize the target environment.</td>
<td></td>
<td rowspan="5">3</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>2. Describe high-level changes in enterprise architecture (business, information, data, applications and technology domains).</td>
<td></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>3. Consider the high-level implications of all gaps. Assess the impact of potential changes on business and I&T operating models, I&T research and development capabilities, and I&T investment programs.</td>
<td></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>4. Consider the value of potential changes to business and IT capabilities, I&T services and enterprise architecture, and the implications if no changes are realized.</td>
<td></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>5. Refine the target environment definition and prepare a value statement outlining benefits of the target environment.</td>
<td></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><strong>Related Guidance (Standards, Frameworks, Compliance Requirements)</strong></td>
<td><strong>Detailed Reference</strong></td>
<td></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>No related guidance for this management practice</td>
<td></td>
<td></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><strong>Management Practice</strong></td>
<td><strong>Example Metrics</strong></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><strong>APO02.05 Define the strategic plan and road map.</strong><br>Develop a holistic digital strategy, in cooperation with relevant stakeholders, and detail a road map that defines the incremental steps required to achieve the goals and objectives. Ensure focus on the transformation journey through the appointment of a person who helps spearhead the digital transformation and drives alignment between business and I&T.</td>
<td>a. Level of stakeholder support for the digital transformation plan<br>b. Percent of initiatives in the I&T strategy that are self-funding (with financial benefits exceeding costs)<br>c. Degree of correspondence between enterprise strategy and I&T strategy and objectives</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><strong>Activities</strong></td>
<td></td>
<td><strong>Capability Level</strong></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>1. Define initiatives required to close gaps between current and target environments. Integrate initiatives into a coherent I&T strategy that aligns I&T with all aspects of the business.</td>
<td></td>
<td rowspan="9">3</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>2. Detail a road map that defines the incremental steps required to achieve the goals and objectives of the I&T strategy. Ensure actions are included to train people with new skills, support adoption of new technology, sustain change throughout the organization, etc.</td>
<td></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>3. Consider the external ecosystem (enterprise partners, suppliers, start-ups, etc.) to help support execution of the road map.</td>
<td></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>4. Group actions into programs and/or projects with a clear goal or deliverable. For each project, identify high-level resource requirements, schedule, investment/operational budget, risk, change impact, etc.</td>
<td></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>5. Determine dependencies, overlaps, synergies and impacts among projects, and prioritize.</td>
<td></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>6. Finalize road map, indicating relative scheduling and interdependencies of projects.</td>
<td></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>7. Ensure focus on the transformation journey. Appoint a champion of digital transformation and alignment between business and I&T (chief digital officer [CDO] or other traditional C-suite role).</td>
<td></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>8. Obtain support and formal approval of plan from stakeholders.</td>
<td></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>9. Translate objectives into measurable outcomes represented by metrics (what) and targets (how much). Ensure that outcomes and measures correlate to enterprise benefits.</td>
<td></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><strong>Related Guidance (Standards, Frameworks, Compliance Requirements)</strong></td>
<td><strong>Detailed Reference</strong></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>ISF, The Standard of Good Practice for Information Security 2016</td>
<td>SG2.1 Information Security Strategy</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>ITIL V3, 2011</td>
<td>Service Strategy, 4.1 Strategy management for IT services</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

<td></td>

<th>Key Culture Elements</th>
<th>Related Guidance</th>
<th>Detailed Reference</th>
</tr>

<tbody>
<tr>
<td>Establish a culture and underlying values that fit the overall business strategy (i.e., customer oriented, innovation driven, product based). Find ways to inject speed into processes and introduce the supporting culture and behavior that allow moving at a faster pace. This could start with changing basic habits such as having more frequent strategy leadership meetings or automating certain activities.<br><br>In the current context of digital business models, ecosystems and disruption, it is vital for many organizations to prioritize digital transformation in their strategy. Build a culture that challenges the status quo and explores new ways of working (e.g., invest in automation to respond rapidly to customers, develop sophisticated reporting and analytics to interpret customer needs, build innovative interfaces to gather customer data, create mechanisms to deliver content and offers across all relevant channels).</td>
<td>The Scaled Agile Framework for Lean Enterprises</td>
<td>Configurable framework that helps organizations deliver new products and solutions in the shortest sustainable lead time (all chapters)</td>
</tr>

## Page 73

# CHAPTER 4
## COBIT GOVERNANCE AND MANAGEMENT OBJECTIVES—DETAILED GUIDANCE

<table>
  <tr>
    <td>
      **Domain:** Align, Plan and Organize<br>
      **Management Objective:** APO03 – Managed Enterprise Architecture
    </td>
    <td>
      **Focus Area:** COBIT Core Model
    </td>
  </tr>
</table>

### Description
Establish a common architecture consisting of business process, information, data, application and technology architecture layers. Create key models and practices that describe the baseline and target architectures, in line with the enterprise and I&T strategy. Define requirements for taxonomy, standards, guidelines, procedures, templates and tools, and provide a linkage for these components. Improve alignment, increase agility, improve quality of information and generate potential cost savings through initiatives such as re-use of building block components.

### Purpose
Represent the different building blocks that make up the enterprise and its interrelationships as well as the principles guiding their design and evolution over time, to enable a standard, responsive and efficient delivery of operational and strategic objectives.

The management objective supports the achievement of a set of primary enterprise and alignment goals:

<table>
  <tr>
    <td>
      **Enterprise Goals**
    </td>
    <td>
      <table>
        <tr>
          <td>
            • EG01 Portfolio of competitive products and services<br>
            • EG05 Customer-oriented service culture<br>
            • EG08 Optimization of internal business process functionality<br>
            • EG12 Managed digital transformation programs
          </td>
          <td>
            <table>
              <tr>
                <td>
                  <strong>Alignment Goals</strong>
                </td>
              </tr>
              <tr>
                <td>
                  • AG06 Agility to turn business requirements into operational solutions<br>
                  • AG08 Enabling and supporting business processes by integrating applications and technology
                </td>
              </tr>
            </table>
          </td>
        </tr>
      </table>
    </td>
  </tr>
  <tr>
    <td>
      **Example Metrics for Enterprise Goals**
    </td>
    <td>
      <table>
        <tr>
          <td>
            EG01
          </td>
          <td>
            a. Percent of products and services that meet or exceed targets in revenues and/or market share<br>
            b. Percent of products and services that meet or exceed customer satisfaction targets<br>
            c. Percent of products and services that provide competitive advantage<br>
            d. Time to market for new products and services
          </td>
          <td>
            AG06
          </td>
          <td>
            a. Level of satisfaction of business executives with I&T responsiveness to new requirements<br>
            b. Average time to market for new I&T-related services and applications<br>
            c. Average time to turn strategic I&T objectives into agreed and approved initiatives<br>
            d. Number of critical business processes supported by up-to-date infrastructure and applications
          </td>
        </tr>
        <tr>
          <td>
            EG05
          </td>
          <td>
            a. Number of customer service disruptions<br>
            b. Percent of business stakeholders satisfied that customer service delivery meets agreed levels<br>
            c. Number of customer complaints<br>
            d. Trend of customer satisfaction survey results
          </td>
          <td>
            AG08
          </td>
          <td>
            a. Time to execute business services or processes<br>
            b. Number of I&T-enabled business programs delayed or incurring additional cost due to technology-integration issues<br>
            c. Number of business process changes that need to be delayed or reworked because of technology-integration issues<br>
            d. Number of applications or critical infrastructures operating in silos and not integrated
          </td>
        </tr>
        <tr>
          <td>
            EG08
          </td>
          <td>
            a. Satisfaction levels of board and executive management with business process capabilities<br>
            b. Satisfaction levels of customers with service delivery capabilities<br>
            c. Satisfaction levels of suppliers with supply chain capabilities
          </td>
          <td>
            EG12
          </td>
          <td>
            a. Number of programs on time and within budget<br>
            b. Percent of stakeholders satisfied with program delivery<br>
            c. Percent of business transformation programs stopped<br>
            d. Percent of business transformation programs with regular reported status updates
          </td>
        </tr>
      </table>
    </td>
  </tr>
</table>

### A. Component: Process

<table>
  <tr>
    <td>
      **Management Practice**
    </td>
    <td>
      **Example Metrics**
    </td>
  </tr>
  <tr>
    <td>
      **APO03.01 Develop the enterprise architecture vision.**<br>
      The architecture vision provides a first-cut, high-level description of the baseline and target architectures, covering the business, information, data, application and technology domains. The architecture vision provides the sponsor with a key tool to sell the benefits of the proposed capabilities to stakeholders within the enterprise. The architecture vision describes how the new capabilities (in line with I&T strategy and objectives) will meet enterprise goals and strategic objectives and address stakeholder concerns when implemented.
    </td>
    <td>
      a. Level of architecture customer feedback<br>
      b. Degree to which the baseline and target architectures cover the business, information, data, application and technology domains and frequency of updates
    </td>
  </tr>
</table>

&lt;page_number&gt;73&lt;/page_number&gt;

## Page 74

# COBIT® 2019 FRAMEWORK: GOVERNANCE AND MANAGEMENT OBJECTIVES

## A. Component: Process (cont.)

<table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th>Activities</th>
<th>Capability Level</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>1. Identify key stakeholders and their concerns/objectives. Define key enterprise requirements to be addressed as well as architecture views to be developed to satisfy stakeholder requirements.</td>
<td>2</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>2. Identify enterprise goals and strategic drivers. Define constraints that must addressed, including both enterprisewide and project-specific constraints (e.g., time, schedule, resources, etc.).</td>
<td></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>3. Align architecture objectives with strategic program priorities.</td>
<td></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>4. Understand enterprise capabilities and goals, then identify options to realize those goals.</td>
<td></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>5. Assess the enterprise's readiness for change.</td>
<td></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>6. Define scope of baseline architecture and target architecture. Enumerate items that are in scope as well as those out of scope. (Baseline and target architecture need not be described at the same level of detail.)</td>
<td></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>7. Understand current enterprise strategic goals and objectives. Work within the strategic planning process to ensure that I&T-related enterprise architecture opportunities are leveraged in the development of the strategic plan.</td>
<td></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>8. Based on stakeholder concerns, business capability requirements, scope, constraints and principles, create the architecture vision (i.e., the high-level view of baseline and target architectures).</td>
<td></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>9. Confirm and elaborate architecture principles, including enterprise principles. Ensure that any existing definitions are current. Clarify any areas of ambiguity.</td>
<td>3</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>10. Identify enterprise change risk associated with the architecture vision. Assess the initial level of risk (e.g., critical, marginal or negligible). Develop a mitigation strategy for each significant risk.</td>
<td></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>11. Develop an enterprise architecture concept business case and outline plans and statement of architecture work. Secure approval to initiate a project aligned and integrated with the enterprise strategy.</td>
<td></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>12. Define the target architecture value propositions, goals and metrics.</td>
<td>4</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

<table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th>Related Guidance (Standards, Frameworks, Compliance Requirements)</th>
<th>Detailed Reference</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>National Institute of Standards and Technology Special Publication 800-53, Revision 5 (Draft), August 2017</td>
<td>3.15 Program management (PM-7)</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>The Open Group Standard TOGAF version 9.2, 2018</td>
<td>6. Phase A: Architecture Vision</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

<table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th>Management Practice</th>
<th>Example Metrics</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>APO03.02 Define reference architecture.<br>The reference architecture describes the current and target architectures for the business, information, data, application and technology domains.</td>
<td>a. Date of last update to domain and/or federated architectures<br>b. Number of exceptions to architecture standards and baselines applied for and granted</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

<table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th>Activities</th>
<th>Capability Level</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>1. Maintain an architecture repository containing standards, reusable components, modeling artifacts, relationships, dependencies and views, to enable uniformity of architectural organization and maintenance.</td>
<td>3</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>2. Select reference viewpoints from the architecture repository that enable the architect to demonstrate how stakeholder concerns are addressed in the architecture.</td>
<td></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>3. For each viewpoint, select models needed to support the specific view required. Use selected tools or methods and the appropriate level of decomposition.</td>
<td></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>4. Develop baseline architectural domain descriptions, using the scope and level of detail necessary to support the target architecture and, to the extent possible, identifying relevant architecture building blocks from the architecture repository.</td>
<td></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>5. Maintain a process architecture model as part of the baseline and target domain descriptions. Standardize the descriptions and documentation of processes. Define the roles and responsibilities of the process decision makers, process owner, process users, process team and any other process stakeholders who should be involved.</td>
<td></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>6. Maintain an information architecture model as part of baseline and target domain descriptions, consistent with enterprise strategy to acquire, store and use data optimally in support of decision making.</td>
<td></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>7. Verify architecture models for internal consistency and accuracy. Perform a gap analysis between baseline and target. Prioritize gaps and define new or modified components that must be developed for the target architecture. Resolve incompatibilities, inconsistencies or conflicts within the target architecture.</td>
<td></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>8. Conduct a formal stakeholder review by vetting proposed architecture against the original intent of the architecture project and the statement of architecture work.</td>
<td></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>9. Finalize business, information, data, applications and technology domain architectures. Create an architecture definition document.</td>
<td></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

&lt;page_number&gt;74&lt;/page_number&gt;

CHAPTER 4
COBIT GOVERNANCE AND MANAGEMENT OBJECTIVES—DETAILED GUIDANCE

A. Component: Process (cont.)

<table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th>Related Guidance (Standards, Frameworks, Compliance Requirements)</th>
<th>Detailed Reference</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>CMMI Data Management Maturity Model, 2014</td>
<td>Platform and Architecture—Architectural Approach; Platform and Architecture—Data Integration</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>ITIL V3, 2011</td>
<td>Service Strategy, 5.4 IT service strategy and enterprise architecture</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>National Institute of Standards and Technology Special Publication 800-37, Revision 2 (Draft), May 2018</td>
<td>3.1 Preparation (Task 9)</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>National Institute of Standards and Technology Special Publication 800-53, Revision 5 (Draft), August 2017</td>
<td>3.5 Configuration management (CM-8)</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>The Open Group Standard TOGAF version 9.2, 2018</td>
<td>7. Phase B: Business Architecture; 8. Phase C: Information Systems Architectures; 9. Phase C: Information Systems Architectures Data Architecture; 10. Phase C: Information Systems Architectures Application Architecture; 11. Phase D: Technology Architecture</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

<table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th>Management Practice</th>
<th>Example Metrics</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>APO03.03 Select opportunities and solutions.<br>Rationalize the gaps between baseline and target architectures, accounting for both business and technical perspectives, and logically group them into project work packages. Integrate the project with any related I&T-enabled investment programs to ensure that the architectural initiatives are aligned with and enable these initiatives as part of overall enterprise change. Make this a collaborative effort with key enterprise stakeholders from business and IT to assess the enterprise’s transformation readiness, and identify opportunities, solutions and all implementation constraints.</td>
<td>a. Number of identified gaps in models across enterprise, information, data, application and technology architecture domains<br>b. Percent of key enterprise stakeholders from business and IT to assess the enterprise’s transformation readiness, and identify opportunities, solutions and all implementation constraints</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

<table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th>Activities</th>
<th>Capability Level</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>1. Determine and confirm key enterprise change attributes. Consider enterprise culture, the potential impact of culture on implementation of architecture and the enterprise’s capabilities for transition.</td>
<td>3</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>2. Identify any enterprise drivers that would constrain the sequence of implementation. Include a review of enterprise and line-of-business strategic and business plans. Consider current enterprise architecture maturity.</td>
<td></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>3. Review and consolidate results of the gap analysis between baseline and target architectures. Assess implications with respect to potential solutions, opportunities, interdependencies and alignment with current I&T-enabled programs.</td>
<td></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>4. Assess requirements, gaps, solutions and other factors to identify a minimal set of functional requirements whose integration into work packages would lead to a more efficient and effective implementation of target architecture.</td>
<td></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>5. Reconcile the consolidated requirements with potential solutions.</td>
<td></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>6. Refine initial dependencies and identify constraints on implementation and migration plans. Compile a dependency analysis report.</td>
<td></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>7. Confirm the enterprise’s readiness for, and the risk associated with, enterprise transformation.</td>
<td></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>8. Formulate high-level strategy for implementation and migration. Implement target architecture (and arrange any transition architecture) according to overall enterprise strategy, objectives and timelines.</td>
<td></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>9. Identify and group major work packages into a coherent set of programs and projects, respecting the direction and approach to enterprise strategic implementation.</td>
<td></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>10. Develop transition architectures where the scope of change required by the target architecture necessitates an incremental approach.</td>
<td></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

<table>
<thead>
<tr>

## Page 76

# COBIT® 2019 FRAMEWORK: GOVERNANCE AND MANAGEMENT OBJECTIVES

## A. Component: Process (cont.)

<table>
  <thead>
    <tr>
      <th>Management Practice</th>
      <th>Example Metrics</th>
    </tr>
  </thead>
  <tbody>
    <tr>
      <td><strong>APO03.04 Define architecture implementation.</strong><br>Create a viable implementation and migration plan in alignment with the program and project portfolios. Ensure the plan is closely coordinated to deliver value and that the required resources are available to complete the necessary work.</td>
      <td>a. Clear definition of architecture implementation governance requirements<br>b. Percent of stakeholders aware of architecture implementation and migration</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td colspan="2"><strong>Activities</strong></td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td colspan="2">1. Establish items required in the implementation and migration plan as part of program and project planning. Ensure that the plan aligns with requirements of relevant decision makers.</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td colspan="2">2. Confirm increments and phases of the transition architecture. Update the architecture definition document.</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td colspan="2">3. Define and complete the architecture implementation and migration plan, including relevant governance requirements. Integrate the plan, activities and dependencies into program and project planning.</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td colspan="2">4. Communicate the defined architectural road map to relevant stakeholders. Inform stakeholders about the target architecture definition, architecture guidelines and principles, service portfolio, etc.</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td colspan="2"><strong>Related Guidance (Standards, Frameworks, Compliance Requirements)</strong></td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td>CMMI Data Management Maturity Model, 2014</td>
      <td>Platform and Architecture—Architectural Approach; Platform and Architecture—Data Integration</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td>The Open Group Standard TOGAF version 9.2, 2018</td>
      <td>13. Phase F: Migration Planning</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td colspan="2"><strong>Management Practice</strong></td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td><strong>APO03.05 Provide enterprise architecture services.</strong><br>Provide enterprise architecture services within the enterprise that include guidance to and monitoring of implementation projects, formalizing ways of working through architecture contracts, and measuring and communicating architecture's value and compliance monitoring.</td>
      <td>a. Level of customer feedback for architecture services<br>b. Percent of projects that utilize the framework and methodology to reuse defined components<br>c. Percent of projects using enterprise architecture services<br>d. Project benefits realized that can be traced back to architecture involvement (e.g., cost reduction through reuse)</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td colspan="2"><strong>Activities</strong></td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td colspan="2">1. Confirm scope and priorities and provide guidance for solution development and deployment (e.g., by using service-oriented architecture).</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td colspan="2">2. Manage enterprise architecture requirements and support business and IT with advice and expertise on architectural principles, models and building blocks. Guarantee that new implementations (as well as changes to current architecture) align with enterprise architecture principles and requirements.</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td colspan="2">3. Manage portfolio of enterprise architecture services and ensure alignment with strategic objectives and solution development.</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td colspan="2">4. Identify enterprise architecture priorities. Align priorities to value drivers. Define and collect value metrics and measure and communicate the value of enterprise architecture.</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td colspan="2">5. Establish a technology forum to provide architectural guidelines, advise projects and guide selection of technology. Measure compliance with standards and guidelines, including compliance with external requirements and internal business relevance.</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td colspan="2"><strong>Related Guidance (Standards, Frameworks, Compliance Requirements)</strong></td>
    </tr>

## Page 81

# CHAPTER 4
## COBIT GOVERNANCE AND MANAGEMENT OBJECTIVES—DETAILED GUIDANCE

<table>
  <tr>
    <td>
      **Domain:** Align, Plan and Organize<br>
      **Management Objective:** APO04 – Managed Innovation
    </td>
    <td>
      **Focus Area:** COBIT Core Model
    </td>
  </tr>
</table>

### Description
Maintain an awareness of I&T and related service trends and monitor emerging technology trends. Proactively identify innovation opportunities and plan how to benefit from innovation in relation to business needs and the defined I&T strategy. Analyze what opportunities for business innovation or improvement can be created by emerging technologies, services or I&T-enabled business innovation; through existing established technologies; and by business and IT process innovation. Influence strategic planning and enterprise architecture decisions.

### Purpose
Achieve competitive advantage, business innovation, improved customer experience, and improved operational effectiveness and efficiency by exploiting I&T developments and emerging technologies.

The management objective supports the achievement of a set of primary enterprise and alignment goals:

<table>
  <tr>
    <td>
      **Enterprise Goals**
      <ul>
        <li>• EG01 Portfolio of competitive products and services</li>
        <li>• EG13 Product and business innovation</li>
      </ul>
      **Example Metrics for Enterprise Goals**
      <table>
        <tr>
          <td>EG01</td>
          <td>
            a. Percent of products and services that meet or exceed targets in revenues and/or market share<br>
            b. Percent of products and services that meet or exceed customer satisfaction targets<br>
            c. Percent of products and services that provide competitive advantage<br>
            d. Time to market for new products and services
          </td>
        </tr>
        <tr>
          <td>EG13</td>
          <td>
            a. Level of awareness and understanding of business innovation opportunities<br>
            b. Stakeholder satisfaction with levels of product and innovation expertise and ideas<br>
            c. Number of approved product and service initiatives resulting from innovative ideas
          </td>
        </tr>
      </table>
    </td>
    <td>
      &lt;img&gt;Right arrow icon&lt;/img&gt;
      **Alignment Goals**
      <ul>
        <li>• AG06 Agility to turn business requirements into operational solutions</li>
        <li>• AG13 Knowledge, expertise and initiatives for business innovation</li>
      </ul>
      **Example Metrics for Alignment Goals**
      <table>
        <tr>
          <td>AG06</td>
          <td>
            a. Level of satisfaction of business executives with I&T responsiveness to new requirements<br>
            b. Average time to market for new I&T-related services and applications<br>
            c. Average time to turn strategic I&T objectives into agreed and approved initiatives<br>
            d. Number of critical business processes supported by up-to-date infrastructure and applications
          </td>
        </tr>
        <tr>
          <td>AG13</td>
          <td>
            a. Level of business executive awareness and understanding of I&T innovation possibilities<br>
            b. Number of approved initiatives resulting from innovative I&T ideas<br>
            c. Number of innovation champions recognized/awarded
          </td>
        </tr>
      </table>
    </td>
  </tr>
</table>

### A. Component: Process

<table>
  <tr>
    <td>
      **Management Practice**
      <br>
      APO04.01 Create an environment conducive to innovation.<br>
      Create an environment that is conducive to innovation, considering methods such as culture, reward, collaboration, technology forums, and mechanisms to promote and capture employee ideas.
    </td>
    <td>
      **Example Metrics**
      <ul>
        <li>a. Enterprise stakeholder perception and feedback on I&T innovation</li>
        <li>b. Inclusion of innovation or emerging technology-related objectives in performance goals for relevant staff</li>
      </ul>
    </td>
  </tr>
</table>

<table>
  <thead>
    <tr>
      <th>Activities</th>
      <th>Capability Level</th>
    </tr>
  </thead>
  <tbody>
    <tr>
      <td>
        1. Create an innovation plan that includes risk appetite, a proposed budget for innovation initiatives and innovation objectives.<br>
        2. Provide infrastructure that can be a governance component for innovation (e.g., collaboration tools for enhancing work between geographic locations and/or divisions).<br>
        3. Maintain a program-enabling staff to submit innovation ideas and create an appropriate decision-making structure to assess and move ideas forward.<br>
        4. Encourage innovation ideas from customers, suppliers and business partners.
      </td>
      <td>2</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td></td>
      <td>3</td>
    </tr>
  </tbody>
</table>

<table>
  <tr>
    <td>
      **Related Guidance (Standards, Frameworks, Compliance Requirements)**
    </td>
    <td>
      **Detailed Reference**
    </td>
  </tr>
  <tr>
    <td colspan="2">No related guidance for this management practice</td>
  </tr>
</table>

&lt;page_number&gt;81&lt;/page_number&gt;
